article_title: Dana-Farber's CAR-PRISM Trial Achieves 100% MRD-Negativity in High-Risk Smoldering Myeloma, Pushing CAR-T Into Pre-Cancer Territory

content_preview.title: Dana-Farber's CAR-PRISM Trial Achieves 100% MRD-Negativity in High-Risk Smoldering Myeloma, Pushing CAR-T Into Pre-Cancer Territory
content_preview.summary: A single infusion of Johnson & Johnson's Carvykti rendered all 20 patients in a Dana-Farber phase 2 trial undetectable for residual disease, the first time CAR-T therapy has been tested in a precursor cancer setting.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A Dana-Farber Cancer Institute phase 2 trial has reported that every patient treated with Johnson & Johnson's Carvykti achieved undetectable disease within two months, according to results [published in Nature Medicine](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-026-04365-y) on April 20, 2026 and presented at the American Association for Cancer Research Annual Meeting in San Diego. The study, called CAR-PRISM, is the first to test CAR T-cell therapy in patients with smoldering multiple ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (841 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis scaffold. Technical detail (MRD 10^-6, CRS grading, NINTs, BCMA mechanism, CARTITUDE-1 context) is accurate and appropriately explained without dumbing down. Caveats about single-arm, single-institution, 15.3-month follow-up, and neurologic signal are surfaced honestly.
source_verification: Nature Medicine (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-026-04365-y) VERIFIED via curl (Nature WebFetch hit a cookie-consent 303; HTML retrieved directly). Title: 'Ciltacabtagene autoleucel in high-risk smoldering multiple myeloma: the CAR-PRISM phase 2 trial'. Published 20 April 2026. Confirms: 20 treated patients, no induction/bridging, CRS 100% grade 1/2 (no grade 3+), NINTs in 7 patients (4 cranial nerve palsies that fully resolved, 3 with persistent grade 1 symptoms), median follow-up 15.3 months, all 20 reached MRD-negativity at 10^-6 by 2 months and remained MRD-negative, no progression/deaths, BCMA-directed cilta-cel, senior authors include Irene Ghobrial and Omar Nadeem (Dana-Farber), data cutoff 11 Feb 2026. All article claims align with the primary source.

STAT News (https://www.statnews.com/2026/04/20/aacr-2026-car-t-trial-results-carvykti-smoldering-multiple-myeloma/) VERIFIED via WebFetch. Publication date April 20, 2026 confirmed. Confirms AACR presentation in San Diego, 20-patient cohort, 100% MRD-negative response, Darzalex as the only approved therapy for high-risk smoldering myeloma, ~20% progression rate on current standard within 5 years, Dr. Ecaterina Dumbrava's 'cure' / 'early immune interception' framing. Article uses STAT as a secondary framing source and correctly flags the paradigm-shift language as hypothesis, not established outcome.

FDA (https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-approves-ciltacabtagene-autoleucel-relapsed-or-refractory-multiple-myeloma) VERIFIED via curl through web.archive.org (direct FDA WebFetch blocked by bot-detection 404). Confirms: ciltacabtagene autoleucel (CARVYKTI, Janssen Biotech) approved Feb 28, 2022 for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma after four or more prior lines of therapy; BCMA-directed CAR T-cell therapy; CARTITUDE-1 ORR 97.9% (95% CI 92.7-99.7). Article's 'approved by the FDA' + 'based on data from CARTITUDE-1 that showed an overall response rate of 97.9 percent' is accurate. Minor nuance: current label is actually for >=1 prior line (post-CARTITUDE-4 expansion), but the original approval and the 97.9% CARTITUDE-1 figure the article cites are both correct as written.

AACR 2026 meeting dates/location (April 17-22, 2026, San Diego Convention Center) cross-verified via aacr.org; matches the article's 'presented at the American Association for Cancer Research Annual Meeting in San Diego'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All load-bearing numbers match the Nature Medicine paper: n=20, 100% MRD-neg at 10^-6, median 1 month to MRD-neg, median 15.3 months follow-up, CRS all grade 1/2 (no grade 3+), 7/20 NINTs (4 cranial/facial nerve palsies resolved, 3 persistent grade 1). 'Six patients >18 months without relapse' is consistent with the paper's 16/20 with >6-month follow-up and longest follow-ups. No induction/bridging design is correctly described. 97.9% CARTITUDE-1 ORR matches FDA label. No hallucinated quotes; Dumbrava 'cure' framing is attributed as a STAT-reported researcher hypothesis, not presented as fact.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism despite the headline-worthy 100% figure. 'What We Don't Know' explicitly flags small n, single-arm, single-institution, short follow-up, cost/access, and the neurologic safety signal. Analysis section presents J&J's commercial interest and the 'cure' question as open rather than resolved. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Archive has adjacent CAR-T coverage — in-vivo CAR-T (UCSF/Berkeley, Azalea Therapeutics, autoimmune) and other oncology items (Rockefeller CD40 antibody, Revolution Medicines daraxonrasib, BioNTech mRNA) — but none cover ex-vivo CAR-T in a pre-cancer/smoldering setting. The CAR-PRISM trial's distinguishing angle (first CAR-T tested in a precursor condition, not active cancer) is genuinely new to the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. Primary source (Nature Medicine CAR-PRISM paper) fully verified; every numeric claim traces back to the paper. Secondary sources (STAT, FDA) are reputable and correctly cited. Framing is appropriately skeptical about durability and generalizability without underselling the result. Ready for publication.
